questions.questionText: The most common cause of preventable blindness worldwide is:
questions.optionB: Cataract (lens opacity — global leading cause of preventable blindness; treatable with surgery — phacoemulsification + IOL implantation)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Cataract = #1 cause of preventable/curable blindness globally. Why correct: Cataract accounts for ~33% of global blindness (WHO). Treatable with surgery. In India: cataract is the leading cause of blindness (~62%). Why A wrong: Glaucoma is the leading cause of IRREVERSIBLE blindness. Why C wrong: DR is the leading cause of blindness in working-age adults. Why D wrong: Trachoma is the leading INFECTIOUS cause of blindness (Chlamydia trachomatis). Clinical relevance: India's NPCB (National Programme for Control of Blindness): targets cataract surgery, school eye screening, vitamin A supplementation. Exam trap: Cataract = preventable/curable #1; Glaucoma = irreversible #1; Trachoma = infectious #1; Vitamin A deficiency = preventable childhood blindness #1 in developing countries. Memory aid: 'Cataract = Curable = most Common cause of preventable blindness.' Reference: Khurana – General Ophthalmology.
